title: Eval orchestrator: data model (sub-project A)
description: Manifest schema for an LLM-eval orchestrator: datasets/versions/examples, targets/versions, graders/versions, run/output/score graph + run metrics. New examples/manifest-evals workspace package. Spec/plan: docs/superpowers/{specs,plans}/2026-06-10-eval-*.
close_reason: Built: 11 entities (record-dot bare-name style, typed IDs, Aeson Value jsonb, UTCTime), relationships, Cascade+Restrict cascades, gin/btree/unique indexes, migration; tests migrate-idempotent + round-trip + cascade + restrict + aggregate + compare-runs. Surfaced+fixed 5 manifest lib gaps (DbType Double/UTCTime, unique index, Manifest.Testing.withEphemeralDb, umbrella exports) + a real GPrimKeyType bug (4+-field balanced Rep) with a regression test. manifest 140/140, eval pkg green.

title: Audit every user-manual page for accuracy + house style
description: Audit each published manual page (docs/*.md, served to the live GitHub Pages site) against the CURRENT code and house style. Recent refactors likely left stale content in pages that were not directly touched: the mkEntity Template Haskell front-end was REMOVED (now deriving via Table), Col was RENAMED to Field (+ Pk/Nullable aliases), the codec became Codec/DbType (ToField/FromField/ScalarMeta GONE), and JSONB columns were added. Per page, verify: (1) accuracy vs current code (no Col/mkEntity/ToField/FromField/ScalarMeta references; correct module/type/function names); (2) code examples are plausibly compiling against the real exported API; (3) house style - NO em-dashes, no other-ORM names (no SQLAlchemy etc.), no marketing/positioning claims; (4) no dead links / stale cross-references. Fix issues found (or file per-page sub-tasks if large).
acceptance_criteria: Each of the 9 pages reviewed and corrected: docs/index.md, docs/getting-started.md, docs/entities.md, docs/relationships.md, docs/cascades.md, docs/unit-of-work.md, docs/migrations.md, docs/queries.md, docs/rls.md. Confirm: grep -rnE 'Col f|mkEntity|ToField|FromField|ScalarMeta' docs/*.md returns nothing; grep -n em-dash returns nothing in published pages; no other-ORM name or marketing claim remains.
close_reason: Audited all 9 published manual pages; fixed stale type PrimKey/Col/ToField refs in entities/getting-started/relationships/unit-of-work, removed Alembic positioning in migrations; acceptance greps clean (no Col/mkEntity/ToField/FromField/ScalarMeta, no em-dashes, no other-ORM names).

title: JSONB: improve projection-annotation ergonomic for ->/->> operators
description: Today (u ^. #userPrefs :: Expr (Json Prefs)) .->> "theme" needs the explicit annotation because #label projections are type-polymorphic (Column a t, t unconstrained by the label - the SP4b entity-agnostic-label design), so GHC cannot pick the JsonbExpr instance. .@> is fine (its literal pins the type). Explore: a typed projection helper, tying labels to the entity's real column types, or a fundep, so ->/->> read cleanly without the annotation. Follow-up to manifest-5uk.
close_reason: Added ?. typed projection: recovers a column's real type from the entity's Generic record (FieldType/FindField families + Symbol-carrying Label), so jsonb ops (.->>/.@>) need no :: Expr (Json T) annotation, and a wrong field name is a compile error (TypeError 'no field named'). Additive - ^. untouched. 135/135 incl. a compile-failure golden.

title: JSONB: GIN index support for jsonb columns
description: Let a jsonb column declare a GIN index so @> containment queries use it. Extend the migration engine (ManagedTable/index reconciliation) to emit CREATE INDEX ... USING GIN (col) and reconcile against pg_indexes. Likely an opt-in marker or a per-entity index declaration. Follow-up to manifest-5uk (JSONB columns).
close_reason: Declarative indexes: Entity.indexes :: [Index a] (defaulted), gin/btree builders (Manifest.Index over #col labels), reconciled by the migration engine via pg_indexes (new Core.Index module, ManagedTable.mtIndexes, MigrationPlan.planIndexes, create-if-absent only - never drops, so the pkey/user indexes are safe). Mirrors RLS. 132/132.

title: JSONB columns: Json a via autodocodec + @>/->/->> operators
close_reason: Json a jsonb column via autodocodec HasCodec; SqlJsonb; @>/->/->> query operators (JsonbExpr/Jsonb); nullable jsonb; 126/126. Completes slice 2 of 2 of 'make the core friendlier'. Projection needs :: Expr (Json a) annotation (polymorphic-label limitation, documented).

title: Codec-primary core: Codec/DbType profunctor + Col->Field
close_reason: Replaced ToField/FromField/ScalarMeta with one profunctor Codec a b + DbType class; renamed Col->Field + Pk/Nullable aliases; deriving newtype DbType + dimap custom columns; 121/121. Slice 2 (JSONB via autodocodec) still pending.

title: Replace Template Haskell with DerivingVia/Generics entity deriving
description: Remove the mkEntity TH front-end (Manifest.Derive.TH, template-haskell dep) and replace it with a fully-derivable Entity via DerivingVia + Generics + DefaultSignatures. PrimKey becomes a standalone type family (computed from the record Rep, no per-entity line); rowDecoder/rowEncode/primKey get generic DefaultSignatures; a Table name carrier supplies the table name so plain entities are one 'deriving via (Table "users" UserT) instance Entity User' line, and cascade/RLS entities write a short explicit instance (table name + policy). Migrate all entities (fixtures, RLS Secret, typed-fields Account/Note, app schema). Design: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-09-deriving-via-entity-design.md.
close_reason: Replaced mkEntity TH with DerivingVia/Generics Entity; standalone PrimKey family, generic primKey, Table carrier; all entities migrated; 116/116; docs updated

title: Typed fields: newtype columns + typed primary/foreign keys
description: Make important fields distinct newtypes for type safety: typed primary keys and foreign keys (a UserId can't be confused with a PostId or filled from the wrong id) and general newtype-over-base column support (Email, Money). Hand-written newtypes made frictionless via deriving. Design: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-09-typed-fields-newtypes-design.md. Slice A (typed-by-convention) approved; relationship-enforced FK<->PK matching (B) and mkEntity auto-gen are follow-ups.
close_reason: Slice A shipped: newtype columns (deriving ToField/FromField/ScalarMeta), typed primary keys, typed-by-convention foreign keys, demo round-trip + compile-failure golden + manual section. Relationship-enforced FK<->PK matching (B) and mkEntity id auto-gen remain as follow-ups.

title: Add support for PostgreSQL Row-Level Security (RLS)
description:
Let Manifest work with PostgreSQL Row-Level Security so multi-tenant / per-user access policies are enforced by the database rather than hand-written WHERE clauses.

Scope to design:
- Session/transaction context: set RLS-relevant settings within a session or transaction so policies apply for that unit of work (e.g. SET LOCAL role = ..., or SET LOCAL <app.guc> = '<tenant>' at the start of withTransaction/withSession). Must be LOCAL-scoped so a pooled connection does not leak context to the next checkout.
- A typed API to declare/set the RLS context (e.g. withRlsContext / a SessionConfig hook) rather than raw SQL.
- Migrations: emit RLS DDL (ALTER TABLE ... ENABLE/FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY, CREATE POLICY ...) from a declarative description, and diff it like columns.
- Connection-pool safety: ensure SET LOCAL / RESET semantics interact correctly with the STM pool in Manifest.Postgres (no context bleed across withConnection checkouts).

Open questions: GUC-variable vs SET ROLE approach; how policies are expressed (Haskell DSL vs raw SQL); interaction with the identity map (rows invisible under RLS must not be cached as 'absent').

## Context
Filed during SP4a (TH front-end). Follow-up feature, not part of SP4. Touches Manifest.Session (withTransaction/withConnection), the STM pool (Manifest.Postgres), and the migration engine (Manifest.Migrate).
acceptance_criteria: A documented way to scope an RLS context (role or GUC) to a session/transaction guaranteed not to leak across pooled connections; migrations can enable RLS and create/diff policies; a test proves a policy hides rows from the wrong tenant within a Manifest session.
close_reason: RLS shipped: typed policy DSL, declarative migration, withRlsContext, e2e tenant-isolation test.

title: Manifest: diffTable is blind to nullability changes
description: liveColumns already fetches is_nullable but diffTable compares only column presence + sqlTypeLive, pattern-matching nullability into _. A NOT NULL -> nullable entity change (e.g. manifest-evals Score.value Double -> Maybe Double) reports UpToDate on a pre-change DB: no additive DDL, no destructive-review abort, then a runtime 23502 on the first NULL insert. Fix: diff cmNullable vs live is_nullable; nullable->NOT NULL belongs in planDestructive, NOT NULL->nullable could be additive (ALTER COLUMN DROP NOT NULL). Surfaced by scoring Task 2 quality review.

title: Eval orchestrator: live progress (sub-project D)
description: Server-to-client push of run progress into the Miso UI. Needs the event-sourcing / LISTEN-NOTIFY change feed (manifest-z8h).

title: Manifest: HasRelation.Target family name clashes with entity types
description: The HasRelation associated type family 'Target' collides with any entity literally named Target (a plausible name). Forced a module split in the eval pkg (Schema.Types + Schema). Consider renaming the family (e.g. RelTarget/Related) so user entities can be named Target.
close_reason: Family renamed to Related (clean break, manifest f59be95); manifest-evals un-split Evals.Schema.Types and dropped all hiding (Target) workarounds.

title: Manifest: recursive (multi-level) cascade deletes
description: Cascades are single-level (Manifest.Session.flushDelete emits one flat DELETE per rule, no recursion, no DB ON DELETE FK). Deleting a Run removes its Outputs but ORPHANS the Scores under them. Add opt-in recursive cascade (walk the child's own cascadeRules) or DB-level ON DELETE FKs. Surfaced by the eval schema (Run->Output->Score).
close_reason: Deletes walk the whole cascade tree: Restrict anywhere aborts atomically (incl. autoflush-pinned ordering); Cascade/SetNull apply at depth via nested IN-subquery scopes; diamond shapes orphan-free; cycle guard documented (one level per self-edge). Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-11-recursive-cascades-design.md

title: Eval orchestrator: Miso dashboard (sub-project B)
description: Miso UI on the eval data model: browse datasets/runs, drill into per-example outputs, compare run A vs B. Reads sub-project A. Needs a JSON API boundary (entity types share via aeson/autodocodec).

title: Eval orchestrator: run orchestrator (sub-project C)
description: Trigger a run from the UI: enqueue, call model providers (concurrency/retries/rate limits), run graders, write outputs+scores, recompute RunMetric. The heaviest piece; model-API integrations + grader execution. NOTE: deleting a Run needs multi-level deletion (Manifest cascade is single-level) - delete scores then outputs then run, or wait on the recursive-cascade feature.
close_reason: Implemented in the manifest-evals repo (github.com/garethstokes/manifest-evals): Evals.Execute (injected LlmRunner, bounded-async executeRun with resume), Evals.Execute.Anthropic (crucible live edge), manifest-evals CLI (migrate/run). Plan: manifest-evals docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-11-eval-run-orchestrator.md. Scoring deliberately deferred (parked pending crucible evals work).

title: JSONB: non-binary json column type
description: Support the non-binary 'json' Postgres type (preserves key order/whitespace) in addition to 'jsonb'. A SqlJson type + a Json-text column wrapper. Low priority - jsonb is preferred for almost all uses. Follow-up to manifest-5uk.
close_reason: Won't do (YAGNI). The non-binary 'json' type's only advantage over jsonb is byte-for-byte preservation of whitespace/key-order/duplicates, which is irrelevant for codec-backed columns (Json a via autodocodec and Aeson a via aeson both canonicalize on round-trip). jsonb is strictly preferable for all codec use cases the ORM supports. Reopen if a concrete raw-JSON-text-passthrough need arises.

title: JSONB: plain-aeson (ToJSON/FromJSON) column variant
description: A jsonb column variant requiring (ToJSON a, FromJSON a) from aeson instead of autodocodec HasCodec, for types that already derive aeson instances. e.g. a JsonB a wrapper as a separate DbType instance. Convenience alternative to Json a; aeson is already in the dep closure. Follow-up to manifest-5uk.
close_reason: Added Aeson a column type: instance (ToJSON a, FromJSON a) => DbType (Aeson a) storing as jsonb via aeson encode/eitherDecode (alternative to Json a's autodocodec). aeson added to depends (already in lock). 128/128.

title: JSONB: path operators #> and #>>
description: Add the jsonb path operators #> (text[] path -> jsonb) and #>> (text[] path -> text) to the query builder, alongside the existing .@>/.->/.->>. Renders col #> '{a,b}' style path arrays. Follow-up to manifest-5uk.
close_reason: Added .#> and .#>> jsonb path operators (text[] path -> jsonb / text) parallel to .->/.->>, with pathLit rendering an escaped Postgres array literal. 128/128.

title: Explore event sourcing / FRP support
description:
Investigate whether/how Manifest could support event sourcing and/or functional reactive programming (FRP) styles, as an alternative or complement to the current snapshot-diff Unit of Work.

Areas to explore:
- Event sourcing: model writes as an append-only event log (events as entities), with derived read models / projections. How would this sit alongside the identity-map + snapshot-diff session? Could 'save' optionally emit domain events? Event store schema, replay, snapshots/compaction.
- Projections: rebuilding read-model tables from the event stream; incremental projection updates (could reuse the migration/SQL machinery). Postgres LISTEN/NOTIFY or logical replication as a change feed.
- FRP / reactive reads: expose query results as time-varying values / streams that update when underlying rows change (e.g. driven by LISTEN/NOTIFY or a polling layer). Relationship to the query builder.
- Scope question: is this a separate library layer on top of the Core, or woven into the session? What is the minimal useful slice (e.g. an append-only event entity + a projection helper) vs a full CQRS/ES framework.

Deliverable of THIS task: a design note / brainstorm (not an implementation) weighing the options, the fit with the existing HKD Core + UoW + query builder, and a recommended MVP slice if worth pursuing.
notes: Design PROPOSAL written to docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-09-event-sourcing-frp-design.md. NOT APPROVED: it captures the brainstorm direction (opt-in ES aggregates; jsonb/aeson events; minimal store; per-aggregate projections; LISTEN/NOTIFY change feed; decomposed into 4 slices, ES core first). Awaiting review/approval before any implementation.

title: RLS: missing-ok current_setting variant (currentSettingOr)
description: RLS policy predicates use one-arg current_setting('app.x'), which errors when the GUC is unset (fail-closed). Offer a missing-ok variant (e.g. currentSettingOr :: Text -> Text -> Expr Text rendering current_setting('app.x', true) with a COALESCE default) so a policy can degrade to 'no rows' instead of erroring when context is absent. Follow-up to manifest-yyb.
close_reason: Added currentSettingOr (missing-ok current_setting with coalesce default); policy degrades to no-rows instead of erroring when context unset.

value: Codec-primary core (manifest-ren, done 2026-06-10): column codec is now ONE profunctor value Codec a b (cEncode/cDecode/cSqlType/cNullable) carried by class DbType a (dbType :: Codec a a), in Manifest.Core.Codec. Replaced ToField/FromField/ScalarMeta (all 3 deleted). Custom columns: newtype = deriving newtype DbType (GND, representational); domain = instance DbType X where dbType = dimap f g (dbType @base); validated = refine+lmap. Combinators dimap/lmap/rmap are LOCAL functions (no profunctors dep, no Profunctor instance). HKD field wrapper renamed Col->Field; aliases Pk a = PrimaryKey (Serial a), Nullable a = Maybe a. Helpers encode (was toField), decodeCol (was field). 121/121. Slice 1 of 2; slice 2 = JSONB columns via autodocodec (not started).

DerivingVia entity (manifest-czu, done 2026-06-09): Template Haskell mkEntity REMOVED. Entities are hand-written HKD records + one 'deriving via (Table "posts" PostT) instance Entity Post' line. PrimKey is now a standalone closed type family (not associated); GPrimKeyType takes Base of the FIRST field's marker (CONVENTION: PK must be first field). Entity has DefaultSignatures (rowDecoder/rowEncode/primKey default; only tableMeta required). genericPrimKey = fromField (rowEncode a !! pkIndex). Table carrier newtype in Entity.hs, its instance in Manifest.Derive. Cascade/RLS entities use a short explicit instance. 116/116.

JSONB columns (manifest-5uk, done 2026-06-10): newtype Json a (Manifest.Json) with instance HasCodec a => DbType (Json a) stores values as Postgres jsonb via autodocodec. SqlJsonb added to SqlType. Query ops .@> (containment), .-> (field as jsonb), .->> (field as text) in Manifest.Query via opaque Jsonb marker + class JsonbExpr. WART: ->/->> on a projected column need (u ^. #col :: Expr (Json T)) annotation (polymorphic label). ZINC: autodocodec-aeson does NOT exist (funcs are in autodocodec core); zinc add autodocodec resolves+builds the 46-pkg git closure. Completes 'make the core friendlier' (slice1=manifest-ren codec core, slice2=manifest-5uk jsonb).
